data.title: WesBanco shareholders approve directors, auditor and equity plan
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

WesBanco ( (WSBC) ) has shared an announcement.

At its April 15, 2026 annual meeting, WesBanco shareholders elected five directors—Louis M. Altman, John L. Bookmyer, Todd F. Clossin, Denise Knouse-Snyder, and F. Eric Nelson Jr.—to three-year terms ending in 2029, and Joseph R. Robinson to a one-year term ending in 2027. Investors also ratified Deloitte & Touche LLP as the independent auditor for the 2026 fiscal year, reinforcing continuity in the company’s financial oversight.

Shareholders approved the 2026 Equity Incentive Plan, signaling support for the company’s long‑term incentive structure for executives and employees. They further backed, on an advisory basis, the 2025 compensation paid to named executive officers, underscoring overall investor alignment with WesBanco’s pay practices and governance approach.

The most recent analyst rating on (WSBC) stock is a Hold with a $38.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on WesBanco stock, see the WSBC Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on WSBC Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, WSBC is a Outperform.

WSBC scores well on improving financial momentum (stronger 2025 revenue and cash generation with moderate leverage) and supportive valuation (mid-teens P/E plus ~4.2% yield). The score is held back by only modest recent returns/profitability versus prior peaks and a mixed near-term technical picture, while the earnings outlook is constructive but tempered by elevated CRE payoffs and acquisition-related expense pressures.

To see Spark’s full report on WSBC stock, click here.

More about WesBanco

WesBanco, Inc. is a financial services company operating as a bank holding company, providing commercial and consumer banking, as well as related financial services. The firm focuses on serving shareholders and customers through a governed board structure and executive management team whose compensation and incentives are periodically approved by shareholders.

data.title: Contact Energy issues NZ$47m in shares to acquire King Country Energy stake
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Contact Energy Limited ( (COENF) ) has issued an announcement.

Contact Energy Limited has issued 4,987,902 new ordinary shares via a placement at NZ$9.42 per share, valuing the transaction at NZ$47 million. The shares have been used as scrip consideration for the acquisition of shares in King Country Energy Limited under a sale and purchase agreement with King Country Energy Holdings Limited and the trustees of the King Country Trust dated 17 April 2026.

The newly issued shares will be subject to voluntary escrow under an escrow deed until 17 April 2027, with limited exceptions allowing transfers to certain wholesale investors. The placement was not underwritten and was not open to existing shareholders generally, indicating a targeted transaction structure focused on facilitating the strategic acquisition rather than broad capital raising from the market.

More about Contact Energy Limited

Contact Energy Limited is a New Zealand-based electricity generator and retailer, listed on the NZX under ticker CEN. The company operates in the energy sector, supplying electricity and related services to customers across the country and managing a portfolio of power generation assets.

To address the change in governance, the board appointed current director and Lead Independent Director Godfrey Sullivan to fill the committee vacancy and simultaneously reduced the overall board size from nine to eight members, effective April 15, 2026, signaling a modest reshaping of its leadership structure without indicating broader strategic or operational shifts.

At Moody’s Corporation’s 2026 Annual Meeting of Stockholders held on April 14, 2026, shareholders elected ten directors to one-year terms expiring at the 2027 annual meeting, with all nominees receiving strong support despite varying levels of votes against. Investors also ratified KPMG LLP as the company’s independent registered public accounting firm for 2026 and approved the advisory resolution on executive compensation, signaling broad shareholder backing for Moody’s governance, audit oversight, and pay practices.

data.title: Smart Powerr Secures Structured Financing Through New Note Agreement
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Smart Powerr Corp ( (CREG) ) has issued an announcement.

On April 10, 2026, Smart Powerr Corp. entered into a note purchase agreement with Streeterville Capital, issuing a secured A-1 promissory note with a $1,050,000 principal amount and closing on $1,000,000 in funding at 8% interest with a two-year maturity. The deal includes prepayment penalties, monthly lender redemption rights starting six months after issuance, and stringent default and penalty mechanics tied to defined Trigger Events.

The agreement also contemplates an additional A-2 note for $1,050,000 and a B note for $8,000,000, with $8,000,000 to be held in a controlled account for subsidiary CREG Holdings, LLC and secured by guarantees and membership interest pledges. To protect the lender, Smart Powerr agreed to restrictive covenants that limit new variable-priced or highly dilutive financings and liens on subsidiary assets, signaling tighter balance sheet constraints but providing substantial structured funding capacity.

Spark’s Take on CREG Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, CREG is a Underperform.

Smart Powerr Corp’s score reflects its challenging financial situation, with zero revenue and ongoing losses being significant concerns. While the company has low debt, its negative cash flow and lack of earnings growth are critical risks. Technical analysis provides some positive signals, but they are insufficient to offset fundamental weaknesses. The valuation further emphasizes the need for strategic changes to improve investor confidence.

To see Spark’s full report on CREG stock, click here.

More about Smart Powerr Corp

Smart Powerr Corp., a Nevada corporation, operates in the energy sector and conducts its financing activities through structured debt arrangements with institutional lenders. The company also utilizes wholly owned subsidiaries, such as newly formed CREG Holdings, LLC in Utah, to manage dedicated capital accounts and secure larger, structured credit facilities for its operations and investments.

Average Trading Volume: 10,307,832

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: $14.85M

See more insights into CREG stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: Lakeland Industries Divests Noncore Lines, Refocuses Core Operations
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Lakeland Industries ( (LAKE) ).

For the fiscal year ended Jan. 31, 2026, Lakeland reported net sales of $192.6 million, up 15% year on year driven by 49% growth in Fire Services, though gross margins compressed and adjusted EBITDA fell amid inflation, tariffs and cost volatility. In the fourth quarter, net sales slipped 1.7% to $45.8 million but operating cash flow improved, and the company completed the March 2026 divestiture of its HPFR and HiViz product lines for about $14 million to simplify operations, strengthen liquidity and refocus on core businesses.

Management highlighted a cost-reduction and simplification program in fiscal 2026, including the August 2025 sale-leaseback of Decatur, Alabama warehouse facilities and the acquisitions of Arizona PPE and California PPE to expand U.S. fire services distribution and rental capabilities. Inventory was reduced by roughly $5.3 million since October, and leadership reported better planning discipline and operating cadence heading into fiscal 2027 as demand in both Fire and Industrial remains healthy.

The completion of NFPA 1970 certifications across Lakeland’s fire portfolio, which had constrained ordering during fiscal 2026, is expected to support improved product availability and will be showcased at FDIC 2026 in April. With new leadership, a streamlined portfolio and strengthened capital position, the company is entering fiscal 2027 targeting more consistent margins, improved inventory efficiency and positive operating cash flow alongside high single-digit revenue growth.

The most recent analyst rating on (LAKE) stock is a Buy with a $19.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Lakeland Industries stock, see the LAKE Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on LAKE Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, LAKE is a Neutral.

The score is held down primarily by weak profitability and negative operating/free cash flow, reinforced by earnings-call risks (sharp EBITDA/margin deterioration and guidance withdrawal). Technicals are broadly neutral with a weak longer-term trend, while valuation remains challenged due to losses; recent leadership/board changes provide only a modest offset.

To see Spark’s full report on LAKE stock, click here.

More about Lakeland Industries

Lakeland Industries, Inc., operating as Lakeland Fire + Safety, is a leading global manufacturer of protective clothing and apparel serving industrial customers, healthcare providers and first responders. The company focuses on fire services and industrial protective products, offering a newly completed head-to-toe portfolio of NFPA 1970:2026 certified gear aimed at expanding its position in the U.S. and international fire safety markets.

For the fiscal year ended Jan. 31, 2026, Lakeland reported net sales of $192.6 million, up 15% year on year driven by 49% growth in Fire Services, though gross margins compressed and adjusted EBITDA fell amid inflation, tariffs and cost volatility. In the fourth quarter, net sales slipped 1.7% to $45.8 million but operating cash flow improved, and the company completed the March 2026 divestiture of its HPFR and HiViz product lines for about $14 million to simplify operations, strengthen liquidity and refocus on core businesses.

Management highlighted a cost-reduction and simplification program in fiscal 2026, including the August 2025 sale-leaseback of Decatur, Alabama warehouse facilities and the acquisitions of Arizona PPE and California PPE to expand U.S. fire services distribution and rental capabilities. Inventory was reduced by roughly $5.3 million since October, and leadership reported better planning discipline and operating cadence heading into fiscal 2027 as demand in both Fire and Industrial remains healthy.

The completion of NFPA 1970 certifications across Lakeland’s fire portfolio, which had constrained ordering during fiscal 2026, is expected to support improved product availability and will be showcased at FDIC 2026 in April. With new leadership, a streamlined portfolio and strengthened capital position, the company is entering fiscal 2027 targeting more consistent margins, improved inventory efficiency and positive operating cash flow alongside high single-digit revenue growth.

Average Trading Volume: 96,889

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Linkage Global Inc ( (LGCB) ) has issued an update.

Linkage Global Inc. disclosed that it raised $500,000 in gross proceeds through a private placement of 833,333 Class A ordinary shares at $0.60 per share, under a Securities Purchase Agreement signed on March 18, 2026. The transaction, previously announced in March, closed on April 14, 2026, modestly strengthening the company’s capital position via equity financing.

The company also reported that on April 15, 2026, it issued 6,000,000 Class B ordinary shares at par value to its chairman, Wu Zhihua, for total consideration of $15,000 as compensation approved by the board. This large grant of low-priced Class B shares to a key insider may significantly affect the firm’s share capital structure and governance dynamics, with potential implications for existing shareholders’ relative ownership and voting influence.

The score is driven primarily by severe fundamental deterioration (sharp revenue collapse, sustained losses, and negative cash flow).
